Our V.P. Alex Torrance invited a very interesting guest speaker to our June lunch.
An officer from the Prescott Police Department will be discussing policing policies and a
variety of other topics. We are also pleased to announce a special guest, recently retired
Prescott Police Chief Debora Black will be in attendance. I hope you will join us to thank
Chief Black for her years of public service.
Two ROTC and one JROTC Scholarships were recently presented. Cadets from
ERAU Army and Air Force as well as a high school junior from Bradshaw Mountain
received the MOAA Awards. The Bradshaw ceremony was in person and I was honored
to be there to present the award. Due to pandemic restrictions, Prescott High decided
to forgo a selection this year.
NAC was a proud co-sponsor of the ERAU AROTC Lumberjack Battalion entry in
the 2021 Bataan Memorial Death March. All 11 of the cadets finished the 26.2 mile
march with 35 lb. ruck sacks. I received a great photo of the battalion and a hand written
thank you note for our sponsorship. A very impressive group of young leaders. This was
NAC had our first information table at an event in over a year, on 01 May at the
Frank Shankwitz Tribute & Celebration of Life Day. We finally got to use our flag themed

Many thanks for all of your time and hard work.

Each year MOAA recognizes achievement in communication excellence with
the Marvin J. Harris Award. NAC submitted an entry for print newsletter. I am pleased
to announce that for the second year in a row, our own Outback Newsletter Editor
Mary Jonas has won the highest award of 5 stars. Congratulations. The Outback is a
high quality publication, and we all appreciate the time and effort you put into making
All good things come to an end. Mary has volunteered a lot of her time for many
years producing our newsletter. She has been our editor since 2012. That is a
tremendous amount of time and commitment. Mary would really like to retire from
the editor position. What better time than after back to back 5 star communications
awards? If you are interested in learning more about this position please contact Mary
In closing I thank you for taking the time to read our newsletter and I am really
looking forward to seeing all of you at the 03 June luncheon. Remember RSVP date is
NAC Board of Directors minutes from 05 May unavailable at press time.

If you are in need of a NACMOAA nametag, Dick is the man to contact. The name tags cost $5.82
each. There is no shipping fee if you pick up your nametag at one of our meetings.
We have lost seven members since my last report, but we have added two and there are two
more who are very likely prospective members. Our current membership is 72. If you’d like to join
NACMOAA now, there is a blank membership application form near the end of this newsletter.
For interested Surviving Spouses and for those interested in becoming a member of the Auxiliary,
there’s a form for that also near the end of the newsletter.
We now have contact info and meeting announcements in the Courier, and in two smaller
— Coffee Talk The Lonesome Valley newspapers and Newspaper.

Arizona SB 1331 Full State Tax Exemption for Military Retired Pay
We have word from Sen David Gowan, sponsor of SB1331, that his for full exemption of retired
pay bill is still alive in budget negotiations now ongoing in the state legislature. However, help is
needed to let the House leadership know this bill is important to our members and all retired
military members in the state. Listed below are the names and contact information for House
leaders on this issue. If your chapter is in one of the districts noted, your emails and calls as
Rep Russell "Rusty" Bowers rbowers@azleg.gov District 25 602-926-3128 House
Rep Regina E. Cobb rcobb@azleg.gov District 5 602-926-3126 Chair
Rep Travis Grantham tgrantham@azleg.gov District 12 602-926-4868 Speaker
Rep John Kavanagh jkavanagh@azleg.gov District 23 602-926-5170 Vice
Rep Becky Nutt bnutt@azleg.gov District 14 602-926-4852 AZ
Rep Gail Griffin GGriffin@azleg.gov District 14 602-926-5895 AZ
Below is a suggested message to send to these members:
I’m asking for your favorable consideration of exempting military retired pay from Arizona
income tax. It is a “Work Force Development Bill” because it’s all about attracting and keeping
Here are some key points for consideration:
1. A retiree joining the military at 18 can qualify for retirement after 20 years. At 38 years old,
the majority of retirees will move into a second career and that income will be fully taxed.
2. A majority of retirees bring with them a spouse who will most likely find employment and
their income will also be fully taxed.
3. Retirees of the Uniformed Services bring with them their own health benefits.
4. Retirees will purchase homes, cars, and other assets that will be fully taxed.
5. Many uniformed services retirees bring with them specialized skills that translate to the
private sector and many will start new small businesses.
6. Uniformed Services retirees are disciplined, dependable, skilled, highly trained, and do not
need long periods of initial training as employees.
7. Considering the technical professions, our military is at the forefront in new technologies and
their training regimen is unbeatable. From aircraft maintenance to cybersecurity, our military
leads the world, and these men and women receive years of specialized intense training that our
A Fiscal Note prepared for SB1359 (Identical to SB1331) is favorable for passage. Here is a
quote “ - We estimate that SB 1359 will decrease annual General Fund Estimated Impact
AZ SB 1331 Full State Tax Exemption for Military Retired Pay
individual income tax revenues by $(48.3) million beginning in FY 2022. However, if more
military retirees are attracted to the state as a result of the exemption, the revenue reduction
could be partially offset from additional tax collections to the General Fund.” Unquote.
Another quote from the Revenue State of Arizona Executive Budget Summary, for FY 2021,
Outlook, Page 17, last two paragraphs: “Thankfully, since then the state’s economy and General
Fund revenues have experienced extraordinary growth under the conservative fiscal management
of the Executive and our Legislative partners. Today, the State of Arizona is in its strongest-ever
fiscal position. It is time for the State to recognize the sacrifice of its more than 53,000 retired
veterans, who receive a lifetime benefit from our nation in the form of a pension, by allowing
them to keep as much of that benefit as possible. Therefore, the FY 2021 Executive Budget fully
eliminates this tax by exempting all pension pay of the Uniformed Services of the United States.
– – This new exemption retroactive to tax year 2020 is expected to save the average pensioner
more than $840 per year in State income tax, reducing General Fund revenues by approximately
Thank you in advance for your help on this important bill. Please contact me if you have
Excerpts from SSP presentation by Sharon Douglas at AZCoC meeting 07 May 2021
• THE TIMETABLE FOR THE PHASE IN OF BENEFITS IS:
• 2021-SBP WILL BE REDUCED BY NO MORE THAN TWO-THIRDS OF DIC, RATHER THAN
THE WHOLE AMOUNT. **THE FIRST OF THESE PAYMENTS APPEARED ON THE FEB 2021
• 2022-SBP WILL BE REDUCED BY NO MORE THAN ONE-THIRD OF THE AMOUNT OF DIC
• 2023-SBP-DIC OFFSET WILL BE ELIMINATED IN TOTAL, ALLOWING FOR FULL PAYMENTS
OF BOTH. THIS WILL BE EFFECTIVE JAN OF 2023 AND PAID FEB 2023.
• **THE MOST IMPORTMENT ACTION SBP RECIPIENTS CAN TAKE IS TO MAKE SURE YOUR
ANNUNITY ACCOUNT INFORMATION, INCLUDING EMAIL ADDRESS IS CURRENT.
• **This is also a good time to update your DEERS information.
• It has been read twice and sent to the Committee on Veterans Affairs.
• **Update-Hearing was held on 4/28/21 but no details published yet
• 1. Increase in the amount of Dependency and Indemnity Compensation for Surviving
• 2. Modify requirements for DIC compensation for Veterans rated totally disabled at time
The full Power Point presentation is at GroupWorks for download.

The Northern Arizona Chapter of MOAA wishes to express
our thanks to the staff of Granite Gate Senior Living for their
continued efforts on the behalf of their residents. We hope
they are all staying safe and well during this pandemic. We
hope to be back with them in 2021.

The Outback Newsletter is published by the Northern Arizona Chapter, which is an
affiliate of the Military Officers Association of America (MOAA). MOAA and its
affiliated chapters and councils are nonpartisan.
MOAA MISSION: To advocate for our military community and connect it to the nation
MOAA VISION: To promote and protect a strong national defense and lead our nation to
honor its commitments to all who serve.

The Barn Events Venue at Mortimer Farms LOCATION:
1484 Old Black Canyon Hwy Dewey-Humbolt AZ 86327
The best way to get to The Barn at Mortimer Farms is Head East on Hwy 69 from Prescott. From DIRECTIONS:
eastbound HWY 69 continue 0.4 miles past the intersection with Hwy 169.Go past the American flag on the left-hand
side of the road. Next you will see a banner that says THE BARN painted on a commercial 18 wheeler trailer. JUST past
this you will turn left into the farm. The Barn is directly in front of this entrance. Parking is to the west side of the venue.
If you get lost or have questions, I will have my cell phone with me. 928 899 6655.
The Buffet is $25.00 per person. The reservation deadline is May 19th. COST:
SIDES: Herb Roasted Potatoes & Baked Beans; Loaded Vegetable Green Salad
ENTREES: Slow cooked Beef & Chicken Sandwiches
DESSERT: Fresh Strawberry Shortcake w/whipped cream topping and Carrot Sheet Cake
BEVERAGE SERVICE: Coffee, tea, water & lemonade
IF YOU HAVE DIETARY RESTRICTIONS, PLEASE ADVISE SHARON DOUGLAS NO LATER THAN May 19th.
PROGRAM: Sargent Ben Scott from the Prescott Police Department.
Sharon must have an accurate attendance count for Mortimer Farms by May 19th.
Please cut on the line below and mail with your check to Sharon Douglas
– TO RECEIVE NO LATER THAN Wednesday May 19, 2021.
NACMOAA address is PO Box 95 Dewey, AZ 86327.
You may call Sharon at (928) 632-5939 to place a reservation if you prefer to pay at the door.
